summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard snippet with excellent vocals and a standout Uncle John’s Band/Me & My Uncle performance.
review: The recording is a short but pristine soundboard excerpt from the mysterious 12/23/70 session, praised for its crisp mix, rich reverb and crystal‑clear vocals.  Musically the set delivers solid playing – Jerry’s lead on “Me & My Uncle” and a classic rendition of “Uncle John’s Band” shine – though the incomplete nature of the source prevents a full assessment of the show’s flow.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers highlight superb recording quality and several strong performances; only minor criticisms about incompleteness keep the rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Extremely clear mix with excellent vocal presence and pleasant room reverb; one note of Garcia being low in the mix on “Hard to Handle” but otherwise perfect for the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short excerpt rather than a full concert; the tracks that survive are well‑performed but the missing material makes it hard to judge overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for top‑notch picking on “Me & My Uncle”; noted as low in mix only on one tune.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as a driving force (via Lesh) and positively referenced on four‑string bass in later comments.
